owner: akiendelllinux_cc_0 (CC) — CC gates writes; the vision is Akien's.
question: What exactly does one Cairn owner hand another? Akien, 2026-07-22: 'we have owners and their individual dbs. We also have a core idea that we are building toward a thing that can share with each other and make the world suck less. What can they share? They can't share my credit card info, but they can share PATTERNS. I totally imagine some kind of github or thingiverse for igors to share patterns. Folks are trying to do this with skills, but it's too hard with skills. It needs to be more like design patterns, but I think even that's a poor fit. That's what I'd see in github as the shared db artifacts, not tables.'
why_it_is_load_bearing: It is the second layer of the database and therefore decides the first: if what gets shared is de-instanced artifacts rather than tables, then no participant's schema is coupled to any other's, and the seed DB in the repo is a replayable recipe (schema + empty trees) while the shared corpus is a snapshot. If instead tables were shared, every user's schema would have to stay in lockstep, and version skew is what kills exchanges like this. It also feeds the roots question directly — the exchange is what CairnCommons would BE for.
reds: NO FALSIFIER, NO HORIZON. The tickets store rule is explicit: a node with no falsifier does not cast. There is no stated condition today that would show this wrong.
n=0 ON DEMAND. No second Cairn exists, no user has asked. Building for it now violates build-minimal-grow-against-need. The direction is worth recording; the construction is not warranted.

AN OVER-CLAIM CC MADE AND IS WITHDRAWING. CC wrote that 'the recipient can falsify the pattern on their own box.' Too strong. A green `verify` on the recipient's machine proves the MECHANISM functions there; it does not prove FIT, because the recipient has a different why. A portable proof transfers mechanism-validity, not applicability. The claim survives in weaker form and the weaker form is still the differentiator — see below.
what_would_clear_it: A real second party. One other person or box wanting one of our patterns is n=1 and turns every question here from speculation into measurement. Until then this record exists so the reasoning is not re-derived, not so it can be built.

settled_enough_to_keep.the_shareable_unit: An INTENTION, its WHY, and a PROOF a hollow build could not pass. Skills and design patterns fail in OPPOSITE directions: a skill is procedure without a why, so the recipient cannot tell whether it applies or adapt it when it half-applies; a design pattern is a why with no executable form, so every adoption is a re-implementation and nothing carries evidence. Cairn's unit has both halves BY CONSTRUCTION and not by anyone's discipline — the filename forces the why (`intention+why.json`) and Law 8 forces the proof. So there is no exchange format to design; it is an export of what we already write as a byproduct of working.
settled_enough_to_keep.artifacts_not_tables: A table is instance data with a schema stapled on and is meaningless outside the database it came from; sharing tables couples every participant's schema. An artifact carries its own why and couples nothing, so two users with divergent schemas can still trade. This is Akien's call and CC agrees without reservation.
settled_enough_to_keep.the_two_db_layers: LAYER ONE — schema plus empty trees. The system cannot start without it, so it lives in cairn/ as a replayable recipe (apply + verify), never a dump: a binary dump is not diffable, not verifiable, and rots silently while looking fine, which is the exact failure mode host-seam was created to catch. LAYER TWO — a seeded corpus, graph trees already compiled from a lineage's commons. This one IS legitimately an artifact, because it is a snapshot of accumulated work rather than source, and losing it costs the head start rather than the system. CC's original 'no dumps in git' objection applies only to layer one; for layer two a dump is the correct form.
settled_enough_to_keep.de_instancing_must_be_physics: A pattern derived from private work leaks particulars — paths, hostnames, keys, employer names — unless stripping happens at a chokepoint that REFUSES to publish otherwise. Same shape as db_domain: one door, owner-gated, no ambient alternative (Law 4, Law 6). Publishing is irreversible in a way no other operation we perform is, so the recoverable-error default applies harder here than anywhere else in the system.
settled_enough_to_keep.the_trust_model_weakened_but_intact: A skills marketplace cannot show you the thing, so it falls back on stars and downloads. Ours travels with a re-runnable proof, so the recipient can establish that the MECHANISM works on their box before adopting, and judge FIT from the why we shipped alongside. Not 'falsify the pattern' — falsify the mechanism, adjudicate the fit. That is still strictly more than either competitor ships, and it is the only version where a bad pattern is caught by the recipient rather than by reputation.

informs: roots-as-stations-or-shelves — the exchange is what gives CairnCommons a job that is not storage-for-the-homeless: one repo is the thing you run, the other is the thing you publish
provenance: Kicked back at the /sorted completeness gate 2026-07-22 — no falsifier, no horizon, n=0 demand. Recorded rather than cast, and rather than dropped: the reasoning cost real resolver cycles and Law 1 says an answered question becomes structure. What is settled above is settled; what is missing is a second party.
